Socially Anti, A stand-up comedy show by Shreeks Comedy is aimed at bringing stuff that people usually do not wish to discuss socially or what some may perceive as anti-social, although it`s just the truth that`s being said loud. We as people are conditioned by the society to be as obedient as possible and not speak out even when things go out of control and we may suffer due to our silence and not take a stand on anything and just go with the majority. With this show, we aim to break this majoritarian overload and the restrictions on society to be in a certain way and to not speak out, etc. etc.This is not a political show, and we do not necessarily hate the govt or say anything particularly negative about the govt, but in the course of the show we may touch upon the subject if need be and address any shortcomings or negativities that we see in the people who are ruling us.This is all about expressing our individuality via our expressions on various subject matters like love, lies, cheating and relationships, and also the general views that we may have on all things associated with our normal day-to-day life.It`s not your typical show in the sense that we do not want to put a tag on the type of content that we do or what you will hear; it`s all about our desire to express things that we want to, and we hope there are many of you who also may have a similar view and may love what we have to say.This show is not for those who easily get offended, and hence we request you to kindly not book tickets if you identify yourself as someone who gets offended easily.Even After The Above Warning If you are in the show, then it`s your responsibility to enjoy the show!Read More

Sri Durga Saptashati has been chanted for centuries. On the 27th of March, it takes the stage.VIBHOOTI is a full-scale live theatrical production drawn from one of India's most powerful sacred scriptures, and it is being premiered in Bengaluru with 15+ performers, live music, and a scale of staging rarely seen in Indian traditional theatre.At its heart is Ramya Vasishta, lead singer and the creative force behind this production, whose voice has shaped every layer of this performance from conception to the stage.This isn't a retelling. The ancient verses come alive here, carried through choreography rooted in tradition and movement arts, original and popular live compositions, and a theatrical vision that treats Indian culture not as something to be only preserved, but as something vital, living, and deeply moving.What unfolds over the course of the evening is a seamless weave of music, dance, and narrative, the kind of performance where you stop thinking and just feel.Experience Indian traditional arts at their most ambitious and alive. This is that evening.Come, let’s transform this gathering into a celebration of life, an experience that is truly larger than life.📅 27th March, 2026 | 6:00 PM📍Chowdiah Memorial Hall, Bengaluru🎟️Use code GOBP5 for an exclusive discount on tickets.Produced by Mandra Cultural Foundation | Outreach Partner: GoBrahma Productions Pvt Ltd.Read More

Listeners’ circle is now back after a pause, more committed to the cause than ever. Listeners’ Circle in associationwith Dialogues now happening again at venue!! Speak your heart out without fear of judgment and listen without judging!  Every story matters. Every life matters. Every feeling matters. But do we share our stories, struggles, and experiences with others openly? What stops us? Isn`t it the fear of judgment? And how often we listen to others patiently without judging them?  Think about how it would feel to take an hour out of your fast-paced lifeand tell your story to people who listen to you without judging? Also, listen to the stories of others without judging.  Listeners` circle, Bangalore is an initiative to make that happen. Started in May 2018 at as a not for Profit Initiative, we have hosted 30+ editions until now, impacting more than 250+ Speakers and 500+ Listeners. Listeners Circle is an initiative to facilitate listening without judgment, hence giving a safespace for anyone to speak their heart out without the fear of judgment. We have done 30+ events over the years, enabling people to voice their stories.The previous editions had some exciting stories to listen to. ​​An Entrepreneur from IIT Madras shared his life-changing near-death experience and how it transformed his complete life.​​A Management Professional shared how has been just ‘Ordinary’ all along his life and how that has helped him and his circles. He articulated that being ‘Ordinary’ is the new difference.​A theatre group founder shared how she bravely stood up to the sexual harassment of a fellow passenger in a bus moving through the middle of the forest in midnight. ​​A thirty-something professional shared how she is in a journey of re-discovering love after five years of separation and being single. ​​Everyone has such stories to share. Do you have something to share? ​​Be a part of the Listener`s circle. Speak your mind without being judged. Speak your mind without any boundaries. ​​Would you like to just listen? We have a place for you too.​​   Read More

Name of the event: A Tale of Dreams and Destinies : A Ballet
Event description: A boy with nothing, a princess with everything, and a kingdom that will never be the same when a lamp chooses to appear. A Tale of Dreams and Destinies is a modern ballet, a full-length dance production where every emotion, every conflict, and every moment of joy is told entirely through movement and music.Step into Khazaanagarh, a city of crowded bazaars, hidden treasures, and secrets buried deep underground. At its centre is Avyaan, a street-smart charmer who stumbles into a destiny far bigger than anything he wished for, and Princess Ziva who refuses her gilded cage. When a Vizier whose ambitions have waited long enough finds Avyaan, an extraordinary ask changes everything.Straight from the courts of Italy and France, classical ballet arrives in Bengaluru in its most ambitious form yet. The Lewis Foundation of Classical Ballet, founded and led by Artistic Director Yana Lewis, has trained over a thousand students across seven centres in Bengaluru since 2006. An affiliate of the Imperial Society of Teachers of Dance (ISTD), London, the foundation stages full-length productions that meet global artistic and technical standards and is passionately committed to making classical ballet a living art form in India.About The Lewis Foundation of Classical BalletEstablished in 2006, The Lewis Foundation of Classical Ballet is India’s leading institution for classical ballet training and performances. Over a thousand students learn ballet across seven centres in Bengaluru and the organisaition stages large-scale, full-length ballet productions that match global artistic and technical standards biennially.As an affiliate of the Imperial Society of Teachers of Dance (ISTD), London, and under the aegis of its Artistic Director, Ms. Yana Lewis, the foundation places Ballet, Jazz, Modern, and other allied dance disciplines at the forefront of its teaching curriculum. The organisation’s performance repertoire includes classical ballets such as Cinderella, Belle and the Beast, and Sleeping Beauty, modern ballets like Oz and Alice, curated performances like A Ballet Soirée and Mosaics of Motion, and original choreographies of Ms. Lewis herself.Emphasising the veracity of the art form, the foundation is passionately committed to bringing classical ballet to the forefront in India and become Asia’s best ballet school and company. Read More
Location (Bangalore only): Prestige Centre for Performing Arts: Bengaluru

The 2-Day Mayurbhanj Chhau Workshop introduces participants to the dynamic traditional dance form Mayurbhanj Chhau from Mayurbhanj district in Odisha. Recognized by UNESCO as an Intangible Cultural Heritage, this mask-less Chhau style combines martial arts, rhythmic movement, and expressive storytelling.During the workshop, participants will learn basic postures, footwork, body movements, and rhythmic coordination used in Chhau dance. The sessions will focus on physical training, cultural understanding, and a short choreography inspired by mythological and nature-based themes.Date: 28th and 29th March, Sat and SunTime: 2.30pm - 4.30pmAbout the TeacherAditya Shrivastava is a choreographer, performer, and teacher specializing in Mayurbhanj Chhau. Trained under renowned SNA awardee guru JANME JAI SAI BABU , he has developed strong expertise in the technique, movement vocabulary, and performance traditions of this dynamic dance form.He has presented Mayurbhanj Chhau at various cultural festivals and artistic platforms, sharing its energy and expressive power with diverse audiences. Through his work, he remains committed to preserving, promoting, and teaching this traditional art form while guiding participants with clear technique, discipline, and creative expression. Who is it for?Students of dance and performing artsTheatre artists and movement practitionersCultural enthusiasts and beginnersAnyone interested in learning traditional Indian dance formsLanguage: English and HindiInstructions to Students: Comfortable clothing that allows freedom of movement (preferably loose or stretchy fabric)Water bottle to stay hydratedA small towelRead More

With every brushstroke, you learn to focus, stay present, and enjoy the moment as it is.Painting also gives you something precious that we often miss in our busy lives: quality time. Whether you spend it with yourself, reconnecting with your inner self, or with your loved ones, creating something together becomes a memory you will always treasure.And the best part? It’s a wonderful way to gift something handmade to the people you care about. Klayzo is an indoor playzone designed for Cognitive, Creative, Emotional, Mental and Physical development of children aged 1 - 8yrs from the House of KLAY Schools.  Designed for little explorers, Klayzo offers over 100 engaging activities including LEGO play, life-sized building blocks, arts & crafts, slides, a weaving corner, puzzles, a cozy reading nook, and plenty of space for active play.   Klayzo is divided into multiple zones to foster exploration and free play:  Build zone: Legos, Troy Blocks, Large connector and blocks, Magnetic block play etc.  Solve zone: Jigsaw wall, Tangrams, Wooden puzzles, Rubik`s cube etc. Art zone: Doodle wall, painting, MDF crafts, block printing, weaving, paper crafts etc. Physical zone: Ball pool, slides, rope tunnel, climbing wall  Calm zone: Storybooks, reading corner, comfy space for kids to relax and unwind  Timings:  Weekdays : 1:30pm - 7:30pm ( 1yr - 8 yrs)  Weekends: 10:00am - 7:30pm ( 3.5yrs - 8 yrs) Duration: 90mins, 180mins  With a thoughtfully designed environment, trained facilitators, calming aesthetics and science-backed play experiences, Klayzo ensures both fun and developmental value. Additionally, parents can wait in the parent lounge or work out of Klayzo with secure CCTV access, making it the perfect place to drop your child off safely while you take a well-deserved break. Klayzo Indoor Playzone & Activity Center Read More

Svapna Vrundavana is a powerful and visually stunning Indian classical dance musical that brings to life the divine legacy of the great saint Vadiraja Tirtha of Sonda Kshetra.Rooted in devotion, mysticism, and legend, the production unfolds like a sacred dream — revealing the extraordinary life of the saint-poet, philosopher, and devotee whose spiritual brilliance continues to illuminate generations. As the story journeys through moments of intense devotion, divine miracles, and unwavering faith, the stage transforms into a living tapestry of history and spirituality.At the heart of the narrative lies the mystical presence of Bhootaraja Rau, the fierce and devoted guardian whose loyalty and divine protection form one of the most dramatic and revered legends surrounding the saint.Blending the elegance of Bharatanatyam and Kathak, powerful music, dramatic narration, and breathtaking choreography, Svapna Vrundavana creates an immersive theatrical experience filled with grandeur, emotion, and spiritual intensity.Presented by Prabhat Arts International, this special performance is also a fundraiser supporting the Kamadhenu Project by Rotary Ulla, bringing together art, devotion, and service for a meaningful cause.Step into a world where faith becomes spectacle, legends come alive, and devotion dances across the stage.Read More

Name of the event: LIVE Classical Concert by Dr. Vidyabhushana
Event description: Event Description – Grand FundraisingMusical EveningLions Club of Bengaluru – Sankalpa proudly presents a Grand Fundraising Musical Evening, a special cultural event dedicated to supporting a meaningful humanitarian cause. This memorable evening will feature a live classical concert by the renowned vocalist  Dr. Vidyabhushana, whose soulful renditions and spiritually uplifting performances have touched the hearts of audiences across India and around the world.Every ticket purchased and every contribution made during the event will help supportcancer patients who need assistance during their Medication journey.This musical event is being organized as a fundraising initiative to support cancer patients, reaffirming the Lions Club’s long-standing commitment to service, compassion, and community welfare. Through this event, Lions Club of Bengaluru – Sankalpa aims to bring together musiclovers, philanthropists, and socially conscious individuals to contribute towards a noble cause while enjoying an unforgettable evening of classical and devotional music. For decades, the Lions movement worldwide has been synonymous with service and humanitarian efforts. Following this proud tradition, Lions Club of Bengaluru – Sankalpa has consistently worked towards improving the lives of people in need through various initiatives including medical support, education assistance, community welfare programs, and charitable activities. Supporting patients battling serious illnesses such as cancer is one of the most important humanitarian responsibilities, and this event is a step toward extending hope, care, and assistance to those who require it the most. Cancer is not only a medical challenge but also a significant emotional and financial burden formany families. Cancer care often involves prolonged medical care, expensive procedures, and ongoing support. Through this fundraising musical evening, Lions Club – Sankalpa seeks to raise funds that will be directed toward providing assistance to cancer patients who are struggling with high costs and medical care. The event aims to create awareness about the importance of community support in helping such individuals and families during difficulttimes.The highlight of the evening will be the mesmerizing performance by Dr. Vidyabhushana, one of the most respected and celebrated voices in the field of devotional and classical music. Known for his powerful voice, deep spiritual connection to music, and ability to connect withaudiences of all ages, Dr. Vidyabhushana has earned immense admiration from music enthusiasts worldwide. With decades of musical excellence behind him, Dr. Vidyabhushana has performed thousands of concerts across India and internationally, presenting a rich repertoire of classical compositions, devotional songs, and traditional Kannada devaranamas. His performances are known not only for their musical brilliance but also for the spiritual depth and emotional resonance they bring to listeners. His music has the rare ability to inspire, uplift, and create a sense of peace and devotion among audiences.During this special evening, attendees can expect a soul-stirring musical journey that celebrates the beauty of Indian classical traditions while spreading a message of compassion and unity. The concert will feature a carefully curated selection of devotional and classical compositions that reflect India’s rich cultural heritage. Accompanied by talented musicians,Dr. Vidyabhushana’s performance promises to be an enriching experience for both seasoned classical music enthusiasts and new listeners alike. Beyond the music, the event represents something far more meaningful. It is an opportunity for the community to come together in the spirit of service and social responsibility. The Lions Club strongly believes that music has the power to heal, inspire, and unite people. By combining the universal appeal of music with a humanitarian purpose, this event aims to create an atmosphere where art and compassion come together for a greater good. Attendees will not only experience an evening of exceptional musical performance but will also be contributing directly to a cause that brings hope and support to those battling serious illness. Events like this demonstrate how cultural gatherings can play a powerful role in supporting social causes. By attending this concert, individuals become part of a larger movement that values empathy, generosity, and community participation. The Lions Club hopes that this initiative will inspire more people to participatein charitable activities and extend their support to those in need. The venue for thisspecial event, ADA Rangamandira,is one of Bengaluru’s well-known cultural auditoriums, recognized for hosting prestigious music and cultural programs. The setting provides the perfect ambience for a classical concert of this stature, allowing audiences to enjoythe performance in a comfortable and acoustically rich environment. The evening isexpected to attract music lovers, patrons of the arts, philanthropists, and members of the community who believe in supporting meaningful causes. It promises to be a memorable gathering where people from different walks of life unite through the universal language ofmusic and the shared goal of making a positive impact.Lions Club of Bengaluru – Sankalpa extends its heartfelt invitation to all music enthusiasts,supporters, and members of the community to be part of this noble initiative.Your presence and participation will not only help make the event successful but will also contribute towards providing care and assistance to cancer & dialysis  who need it most.Come and experience an inspiring evening filled with soulful music, cultural richness, and a spirit of compassion. Let the melodies of classical and devotional music create an atmosphere of hope and positivity, while together we take a meaningful step toward supporting those facing difficult health challenges. Join us for this special Grand Fundraising Musical Evening and be part of an event where music meets humanity. Your support can make a real difference in the lives of cancer patients and their families. Be present for an unforgettable night of music, generosity, and community spirit—an evening that celebrates the power of art tobring people together for a noble cause.Read More

Hello, I’m Nishi. I value life deeply and strive to create an atmosphere where people feel happy and fulfilled. My passions include dancing, music, reading, hiking, solo travel, and connecting with new people through their stories. I am naturally broadminded, non-judgmental, and an attentive listener.I’m not a counselor, therapist, or psychologist — just someone curious, kind, and genuinely friendly.I’m also the founder of Odyssey Vibes, a Wellness and Hospitality Community dedicated to creating meaningful connections, nurturing well-being, and celebrating the joy of shared experiences.── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──What’s this Session All About?A one-on-one safe space (not group) with me, designed to help you share, reflect, and feel at ease.In today’s fast-paced world, finding someone who truly listens—with empathy, patience, and without judgment—has become rare. Many of us feel misunderstood, judged, or ignored, which can make us withdraw and choose solitude.Being alone for a while is fine, but lasting connection and support are what we all need.The goal is simple: to help you feel lighter, calmer, and more connected. ── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──Where do we meet?We can meet at a nearby café, mall, or park—or simply take a walk together, whichever feels most comfortable for you.── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──What should you take care about?» You’re welcome to share anything on your mind—just kindly keep the conversation thoughtful and respectful.» Listening deeply to emotions can be intense for me too. After a while, it may be harder to stay fully connected, so I kindly ask you to be mindful of the time. Since feelings often flow endlessly, it’s not easy for me to interrupt—keeping the time slot in mind helps us both remain present and balanced.── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──What if I need further help?Our connection doesn’t end with the session. My focus is always on helping you feel lighter, better, and more at ease. You’ll also become part of the Odyssey Community, where you can meet and connect with wonderful people. And who knows—within that circle, you may even find someone special who brings added meaning and value to your journey.Read More

Name of the event: Melody in Motion - An evening of carnatic violin
Event description: Melody in Motion is an evening dedicated to the art of Carnatic violin, presented by Soundstack in association with Artkhoj. At the heart of this concert is a rare and captivating violin duet by Vidwan G Ravi Kumar, a seasoned artist from the Mysore, known for his rich tonal depth, and Vidhushi Aishwarya Lakshman, a rising talent from Bengaluru celebrated for her perfectness.    Together, they bring a flowing conversation of swara and laya to the stage — an interplay that is both meditative and exhilarating. Accompanying them is Vidwan Balachandra Achar on the mridangam, whose rhythmic precision and dynamic range add a powerful foundation to the concert, and Som Sankar Naskar on percussion, whose versatile accompaniment weaves seamlessly into the Carnatic idiom. Set in an intimate, curated art space at Namma Ashram in Whitefield, Bengaluru, this is not just a concert — it is an invitation to experience the beauty, emotion, and depth of South Indian classical music in a setting where every note reaches you.Whether you are a seasoned rasika or someone discovering Carnatic music for the first time, Melody in Motion promises an evening that stays with you long after the last note fades.Read More

Name of the event: LEARN KEYBOARD IN 90 MINS - UNWIND WITH KEYS
Event description: “Keyboard Unwind: A Comprehensive 90-Minute Workshop”Workshop Overview: Embark on a transformative journey into the world of keyboard mastery with our 90-minute workshop. From understanding the parts of a keyboard to playing songs, this workshop covers essential topics including the chromatic scale, music theory, forming chords, and applying them melodically. Whether you’re a novice or seeking to refine your skills, join us to unlock the full potential of your musical journey.Workshop Agenda:Introduction to the KeyboardExplore the anatomy of a keyboard, including keys, octaves, black and white keys.Discuss the importance of proper posture and hand positioning for efficient playing.Chromatic Scale Exercises:Define the chromatic scale and its significance in understanding musical intervals.Demonstrate fingering techniques for ascending and descending chromatic scales.Practice exercises to familiarize participants with the chromatic scale across the keyboard.Music Theory Basics:Introduce fundamental music theory concepts such as rhythm, tempo.Explain the concept of intervals, scales, and how they relate to keyboard playing.Provide simple examples to illustrate key music theory principles.Forming Chords:Define what chords are and how they are constructed from scales.Explore major, minor chords and their basic structures.identify chords on the keyboard.Playing Chords:Demonstrate various chords and create different textures and moods.Practice transitioning smoothly between chords using proper fingering techniques.Offer tips on hand coordination and maintaining rhythm while playing chords.Playing Songs :Select simple songs or melodies to apply the concepts learned during the workshop.Break down songs into manageable sections, focusing on chord progressions and melody lines.Encourage participants to play along and experiment with improvisation.Integration and Application:Emphasize the importance of regular practice and patience in mastering keyboard skills.Provide guidance on developing a structured practice routine tailored to individual goals and abilities.Q&A and Closing Remarks:Ask questions and share their insights and experiences.Additional resources for further learning and explorationWorkshop Materials Needed:Keyboards for each participant.Digital content for key concepts and exercises covered during the workshop.Digital contet to illustrate keyboard anatomy, scales, chords, and music theory concepts.Song sheetsto practice with.By the conclusion of this workshop, participants will have gained a deeper understanding of keyboard fundamentals, including the chromatic scale, music theory, chord formation, and song playing techniques. With dedication and practice, they’ll be well-equipped to continue their musical journey with confidence and proficiency. Let’s embark on this musical adventure together!Read More

From moments of deep spiritual awakening to divine grace that touched countless lives, the stage comes alive with emotion, devotion, and grandeur.Blending the elegance of Bharatanatyam and Kathak, dramatic narration, stunning costumes, and evocative visuals, Tapohamsa creates a powerful theatrical experience that celebrates the life, philosophy, and eternal presence of Rayaru.The phrase “Rayaridare” reflects the deep faith of devotees — the belief that wherever his name is remembered with devotion, Rayaru is there to guide and bless.Presented by Prabhat Arts International, this special performance is also a fundraiser supporting the Kamadhenu Project by Rotary Ulla, helping empower communities and sustain meaningful social initiatives.Experience an evening where devotion, art, and purpose unite on stage.Read More

ABOUT THE EVENT​Organised by The Builders Club in association with Dell and AMD, the Bangalore Tech Symposium is a one-day gathering for the people who are leading the curve in tech — brought together to talk about the one thing reshaping every business in this space: AI.​BTS brings together four vantage points that rarely share a room:​the CTO rebuilding from the inside,​the GCC operating at global scale and defining what enterprise AI looks like,​the investor tracking where durable value is actually being created,​and the founders and business heads navigating all three forces at once.​One day. The full picture. The conversations that matter.​​AGENDA​Four sessions. One through-line: how AI is changing the way tech businesses operate, compete, and grow.​🎤 FIRESIDE WITH DELL​Rethinking Workplace Computing: What Modern Tech Teams Actually Need from Their Devices​AI workloads are unlike anything legacy infrastructure was built to handle. This opening fireside — held in partnership with Dell — gets into the computing and hardware realities that most organisations are only beginning to confront: why legacy device setups are creating hidden performance ceilings, and what truly AI-ready infrastructure looks like for high-performance tech teams.​💻 TECH PANEL​Building an AI-First Workplace: Infrastructure, Processes and Strategy​The CTO perspective. A practitioner-led conversation with the technical leaders who are rebuilding organisations for AI — rearchitecting infrastructure, overhauling engineering processes, and making the hard calls on where to invest and what to leave behind. This panel goes past strategy and into the operational reality of building AI-first at scale.​🏢 GCC PANEL​The AI Productivity Multiplier: How AI Is Reshaping Processes, Talent and Scale in Global Capability Centres​The GCC perspective. Global Capability Centres aren't just adopting AI — they're defining what enterprise-grade AI deployment looks like for the rest of the industry. Operating across complex, global organisations, GCC leaders are setting requirements, redesigning processes, and making the infrastructure and talent decisions that will shape enterprise AI for years to come. This panel surfaces what's working, what isn't, and what the broader tech industry should be watching.​📈 INVESTOR PANEL​Investing in the AI Era: Where Capital Is Flowing and What It Means for Tech Businesses​The investor perspective. AI is reshaping investment theses as fast as it's reshaping businesses. This panel brings together investors actively deploying capital in the AI space to share where real value is being created, what's getting funded, and how they're reading the landscape. For every founder and business leader in the room, this is a rare, unfiltered view of the forces shaping the ecosystem around them.​🤝 NETWORKING DINNER​Close the day with the people who were in the room. Curated networking with cocktails.​​WHO SHOULD ATTEND​🎯 Built for the Leaders Who Are Already in the Arena​The day is designed for decision-makers who don't have the luxury of waiting to see how the AI shift plays out. If AI is changing how you build, operate, invest, or compete — this event was built for you.​​💻 IT & Technology Leaders​CTOs, VPs of Engineering, IT Heads, and infrastructure decision-makers navigating AI adoption from the inside — rebuilding systems, managing technical debt, and making platform bets that will define their organisation's AI capability for years.​🏢 GCC Leaders & Enterprise Tech Heads​Leaders running or building Global Capability Centres who are deploying AI at enterprise scale, setting the requirements, and shaping what it looks like for the broader industry.​🚀 CEOs, Founders & Business Heads — IT & SaaS​Founders and business heads in the IT and SaaS space who need the full ecosystem view — from infrastructure to investment — to make the right strategic decisions right now.​📈 Investors & Venture Partners​Investors deploying capital in tech and AI who want ground-level signal from the CTOs, GCC leaders, and founders who are building.​​WHAT YOU GET​🌟 Why You Shouldn't Miss This​•        🔭 The complete picture — CTO, GCC, and investor perspectives on AI in tech, in one room, in one day​•        🖥️ First-hand insight from Dell on the device and infrastructure decisions that define AI-ready organisations​•        🎙️ Direct access to speakers and panellists who are leading AI transformation inside real tech businesses​•        💰 An investor-eye view of where capital is flowing in AI — and what it signals for businesses building now​•        🤝 Peer conversations with IT, GCC, and business leaders navigating the exact same Name of the event: Kids Summer Baking Camp: Better Baking edition
Event description: This summer, give your child more than just an activity. Give them a space where they can explore food hands-on, build real skills, and feel proud of what they create.At Tiny Chef Club, children do more than just baking, they touch, taste, question, and understand what goes into their food, in a way that feels joyful, safe, and deeply engaging.What this camp offersA thoughtfully designed 5-day baking experience for children aged 5+, where children are encouraged to be hands-on from the very beginning.Over the 5 days, every child will:•Bake from scratch every single day•Work directly with ingredients by measuring, mixing, cutting, rolling, shaping, and baking.•Understand the role each ingredient plays in a recipe•Explore ingredients like millets, flours, sugars,fats (oil & butter), vegetables & chocolate•Build a more mindful and confident relationship with food through hands-on experienceAll recipes are 100% vegetarian, and the experience is designed to be engaging, sensory-rich, and confidence-building.What your Tiny Chef will Do each dayEach day blends hands-on baking, ingredient exploration, and sensory learning, so children stay engaged while building real skills and confidence in the kitchen.DAY 1: COOKIES 🍪A gentle introduction to grains through baking, where children make millet cookies and classic sugar cookies, and decorate them with royal icing.DAY 2: PIES & JAM 🍯A hands-on day with fruits and baking,where children make fresh jam andlearn to roll, shape, and bake theirown Pie dough.DAY 3: SAVOURY BREADS🍕A hands-on day with dough, where children make their own bread and pizzas, while exploring how yeast helps dough rise. They also discover different vegetables, learn how they nourish our bodies.DAY 4: CHOCOLATE TREATS 🍫An introduction to chocolate, where children learn about its journey from bean to bar and create their own chocolate treats.Day 5: Bake & Decorate Your Own CakeChildren work with cake batter and frosting, baking from scratch and designing their own cake with creativity and confidence.BEYOND BAKINGThrough storytelling, tasting, and guided exploration, children begin to:• Experience ingredients using all their senses• Build awareness around grains, fruits, vegetables,      and everyday ingredients• Develop a more mindful and positive relationship with food• Become more open and curious towards trying new foodsAlong the way, they also build:1. Confidence in their abilities2. Focus and patience through hands-on work3. All of this is nurtured in a supportive, struct environment age appropriately designed specifically for children.Problem-solving skills through real-time learning4.A growing sense of independence and comfort in the kitchen.All of this is nurtured in a supportive, structured environment age appropriately designed specifically for children.The Tiny Chef Club experienceThe Tiny Chef Club is a dedicated culinary learning space for children, designed to nurture curiosity, connection, and confidence through food. We help children go beyond simply consuming food by encouraging them to understand, explore, and engage with it meaningfully, while building skills, awareness, and independence in a warm environment.Each session is designed to be engaging and immersive, with a natural rhythm of baking, tasting, exploring, and creating. Our curriculum is thoughtfully designed to be age-appropriate, ensuring children feel comfortable, capable, and confident as they learn. With small group sizes and mixed-age classrooms, every child receives the guidance they need while benefiting from a shared learning space.About the InstructorThe camp is led by Chef Siri, Children’s Culinary Instructor at The Tiny Chef Club. With a background in pharmacy and professional culinary training, she brings together scientific understanding and practical kitchen expertise.Her teaching style is warm, patient, and deeply child-focused. She works closely with each child, guiding them step-by-step while creating a space where they feel comfortable, confident, and excited to learn.JOIN THE TINY CHEF CLUBIn our kitchen, food is not merely sustenance; it’s a profound teacher, a cultural compass, and a silent storyteller. What children experience early on often shapes how they see food for years to come.This camp is designed to give them a space to explore, understand, and feel confident around food, in a way that stays with them. If this feels like something you would love your child to experience, we would be happy to have them join us this summer.Read More

Step into the mesmerizing world of Lippan Art, a traditional mud and mirror work that has adorned the homes in Kutch, Gujarat, for centuries. This hands-on workshop invites participants to explore the beauty and simplicity of Lippan Art, also known as Mud and Mirror Work. Perfect for those who appreciate the charm of rustic art forms, this workshop will guide you through creating your own piece of Lippan artistry, blending natural materials with intricate designs to bring a touch of Kutch`s cultural heritage into your space.Event Highlights:Introduction to Lippan Art: Delve into the origins and cultural significance of this traditional art form, learning about its roots in the arid landscapes of Kutch and its role in decorating village homes.Materials and Techniques: Discover the traditional materials used in Lippan Art, including clay, cow dung, and mirrors, and how these elements are combined to create captivating designs. Participants will use eco-friendly alternatives to replicate the traditional process.Hands-On Creation: Under the guidance of experienced artisans, you’ll craft your own Lippan Art panel. Learn to shape mud into various forms, embed mirrors, and apply decorative finishes to your work.Design Inspiration: Explore traditional and contemporary designs, understanding the symbolism behind patterns and how they’re used to tell stories or evoke the natural world.Display and Care: Receive tips on how to display your Lippan Art piece and maintain its beauty over time, ensuring you can enjoy your creation for years to come in the hands-on creative activity.Who Should Attend?This workshop is ideal for art enthusiasts, cultural explorers, and anyone looking to engage in a hands-on creative activity. No prior experience in art is required—just a willingness to get your hands dirty and enjoy the process of creating something beautiful!Read More

A musical tribute to the Bollywood Villains who contributed to Bollywood cinema.  The villains of Bollywood are iconic characters who have significantly impacted Indian cinema through their compelling performances and memorable portrayals. Their presence in the movies gave rise to wonderful memorable songs and they were picturised on them. Their dialogues are being quoted and referenced for decades, cementing their legacy in Indian cinema.  Some of the most iconic Bollywood villains include Pran, Amjad Khan, Amrish Puri, Danny Denzongpa, Sanjay Dutt, and more. They are remembered for their powerful performances that defined evil on silver screen and added depth to the films they were in.  A new generation of actors has continued the tradition with both frightening and complex antagonists who range from terrifying and sadistic to subtly manipulative ones, leaving a mark on the audience. Behind every super hero is a super villain; and the villains in cinema add crucial conflict and drama, making them essential to a film. Their presence often defines the hero's journey and elevates the stakes of the story. Portrayal of villains has evolved, with some classic villains being purely menacing, while later antagonists, added complexity and depth, sometimes even making the audience sympathize with their motivations. The event is being presented by the singing sensation Priya Karthik née Mahadevan – once a singing sensation in Mumbai, but now based in Bengaluru. Accompanying her are the established singers – Aishwarya Kasinathan, Aniket, Yaseen, and Niyazuddin with music arrangement by Senthil Kumar.Read More

URUVAM – Rooted Motion A Folk & Contemporary Movement Practice. URUVAM (உருவம்) means form or body shape. The practice is rooted in the idea that the body is shaped by rhythm, labour, culture, environment, and lived memory. URUVAM is a structured movement training that bridges traditional Tamil folk vocabularies with contemporary dance techniques. It explores how grounded folk rhythm and percussive footwork can coexist and evolve within contemporary flow, floor work, and mobility-based practices. Rather than treating folk and contemporary as separate forms, URUVAM approaches them as interconnected physical languages. The sessions are rhythm-driven and physically engaging, combining cultural understanding with technical training through exploration of Tamil folk rhythms and musical structures, traditional footwork patterns and grounded stance work, understanding the cultural and historical contexts of folk songs, study of how everyday labour and community practices shape physicality, and development of rhythmic precision and endurance. The practice integrates contemporary elements including floor work and movement sequencing, flow-based transitions and spatial awareness, mobility and flexibility training, strength and conditioning through movement phrases, and improvisation rooted in rhythmic structures. URUVAM emphasizes rhythmic awareness, ground connection and weight distribution, stamina and coordination, fluidity in transitions, integration of percussive and flowing movement qualities, and cultural sensitivity within contemporary exploration. The practice supports participants in developing both physical technique and embodied awareness of traditionJunafar JunoPerformer | Choreographer | Movement ArtistJunafar Juno is a movement artist rooted in Tamil folk traditions and contemporary performance practice. A graduate in Tamil Literature, his artistic inquiry draws from lived cultural memory, community narratives, and the embodied knowledge of labour and rhythm.He holds a Diploma in Movement Arts and Pedagogy from Attakkalari Centre for Movement Arts, Bangalore, where he trained in contemporary movement techniques, improvisation, body conditioning, and performance-making methodologies.With over two years of experience in theatre and movement, Junafar’s work bridges traditional Tamil folk vocabularies with contemporary physical language. His choreographic practice explores how rhythm, footwork, and grounded movement can evolve into contemporary expression while preserving cultural integrity.He is the director of Alaikkadal, a folk-contemporary exploration rooted in the Karaivalai fishing community of Tamil Nadu, reflecting his commitment to representing lived histories through movement.Through his practice and teaching, Junafar creates spaces where tradition and contemporary form coexist, encouraging dancers to engage with rhythm, body awareness, and cultural depth.Currently based in Bangalore, he continues to choreograph, perform, and develop movement-based practices grounded in identity, research, and physical explorationSpecific Instructions• Open to beginners and intermediate dancers• No prior dance experience required.• The practice is designed as a beginner-friendly and exploratory space for those interested in learning and experiencing folk and contemporary movement forms.• Sessions are playful, engaging, and physically active.• Comfortable clothing that allows free movement and stretching.Read More

Name of the event: Beginners Guide to the AI Drone Journey
Event description: Most people learning drones either waste months bouncing between YouTube videos or drop Rs 30,000 on courses that are 90% slides and 10% "here's a demo."We skip all of that.You walk in with zero experience. Eight hours later, you've built a real drone from loose parts and flown it yourself.How the day worksYou get actual drone components - not a toy kit. We're talking Rs 1,50,000 worth of flight controllers, motors, ESCs, GPS modules, the whole stack. You wire it, configure it, troubleshoot it, and fly it. If something breaks, you fix it. That's the point.Groups are capped at 8 people so nobody's standing in the back watching someone else solder.What sticks with you afterYou'll know how to assemble a drone from parts, how the electronics and wiring actually connect, how AI features like obstacle avoidance and GPS navigation work under the hood, and where things typically go wrong and how to fix them.You also get a free Udemy course for the stuff that takes longer to sink in.Why people care about this skillFreelance drone work pays Rs 5,000 to 15,000 per project. Knowing how to repair your own gear saves a ton over time. And if you're in robotics, IoT, or any tech role, this knowledge carries over directly. At the very least, you stop overpaying for pre-built stuff you could build better yourself.What past participants said"Finally understood what I was actually buying when I looked at drone specs.""Saved me from making expensive mistakes on my first build.""Got confident enough to start taking on small commercial projects."The detailsOne day. 10 AM to 6 PM. 3 spots left in this batch. After this, the next one is 3 months out.Read More

Name of the event: Sangamam ft  Avartanam by Ramaa Venugopalan
Event description: Sangamam was created out of the passion of the Shibulal Family Philanthropic Initiatives (SFPI) to promote Indian classical music, dance and folk traditions, bringing them closer to audiences who wish to reconnect with the depth and beauty of our cultural heritage through meaningful artistic experiences.In this edition, Sangamam presents Āvartanam – The Eternal Cycle of Srishti, Sthithi and Pralaya, an immersive presentation from Anvaya – The Connection Series, conceptualised and performed by Ramaa Venugopalan.Anvaya – meaning connection – explores the interconnectedness of Bharatiya artistic traditions. Through the confluence of Bharatanatyam, Kolam (Rangoli) and Kshetram (sacred temple spaces), the performance reveals how these art forms share a common visual, philosophical and spiritual language.Drawing from the sacred landscape of the Kanchi Kshetram, the presentation reflects the eternal cosmic cycle of creation, sustenance and dissolution through the three presiding deities. Kamakshi, Ekambreshwarar and Varadaraja.With over three decades of Bharatanatyam practice and the creation of more than 3000 unique kolams, Ramaa Venugopalan brings together movement, visual design and sacred geography in a seamless narrative.Through kolam patterns inspired by temple iconography and Bharatanatyam pieces exploring the abstract, the devotional and the philosophical, Āvartanam invites audiences to witness the rhythm of the cosmos , where creation emerges within, desire takes form, and surrender becomes the final return.Read More

A personal branding workshop  should guide participants through self-awareness, strategy development, and practical execution to build an authentic professional identity. This suggested outline is structured for a day intensive or 1 hour session format, drawing from established frameworks like self-audits, storytelling, and digital presence strategies.Workshop ObjectivesEquip participants with tools to define, articulate, and amplify their unique value in professional networks. Focus on actionable steps for career advancement, networking, and online visibility.Session 1: Introduction to Personal BrandingDefine personal branding as the intentional projection of your skills, values, and personality.Explore real-world examples of successful brands (e.g., thought leaders on LinkedIn).Discuss benefits like career opportunities and audience trust.Session 2: Self-Discovery and AuditConduct personal SWOT analysis (strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, threats).Identify core values, passions, strengths, and unique value proposition (UVP).Perform a brand audit: Review current online presence and feedback from peers.Session 3: Crafting Your Brand StoryDevelop elevator pitch, bio, and narrative arc (origin, journey, future vision).Practice storytelling techniques for authenticity and engagement.Create a personal brand statement.Session 4: Visual and Digital IdentityDesign visual elements: Logo, color palette, professional imagery.Optimize profiles on LinkedIn, personal websites, and social platforms.Build a media kit with headshots, taglines, and portfolio samples.Session 5: Online Presence and Content StrategyStrategies for consistent posting, content curation, and thought leadership.Leverage platforms like LinkedIn, Twitter/X, and Instagram for networking.Monitor digital footprint and handle reputation management.Session 6: Networking and ActivationTechniques for building relationships online and offline.Hands-On Activities and DeliverablesGroup critiques of personal brand audits.Individual brand book or one-pager creation.Peer feedback sessions on pitches and profiles.Assessment and Next StepsParticipants present a final brand portfolio. Provide templates for ongoing tracking, such as monthly content calendars.Read More

Name of the event: Somatic Yoga for Deep Regulation & Calm
Event description: This is an exploratory and restorative somatic workshop drawing on neuroscience, yoga therapy techniques, and traditional yogic philosophy. Designed to cultivate deep ease and support nervous system regulation, the session blends slow, mindful movement, breathwork, meditative awareness, and gentle yogic postures to release physical tension and promote mental clarity. The emphasis is on listening to the body, cultivating self-awareness, and experiencing profound relaxation, rather than achieving postural perfection.This workshop is suitable for all levels, particularly anyone looking to reconnect with their body and mind, enhance inner balance, and explore practical tools for nervous system regulation in a safe, inclusive, and trauma-informed environment.Anisha Kotecha is a London-based yoga teacher specialising in mental health. Her work blends contemporary science with traditional yogic wisdom to support holistic wellbeing. Holding a degree in Biology, she integrates insights from human physiology and neuroscience into her teaching, offering practises that are both evidence-informed and deeply experiential. Her sessions combine yoga, somatic movement, breathwork, meditation and sound to support greater harmony. With extensive experience spanning multiple corporate sectors (including brands like WeWork and Radisson Hotel Group), private clients, gym and studio settings, Anisha has guided a diverse range of clients toward mind-body balance. She creates spaces that honour yoga techniques as a practise of self-awareness and inner transformation.Instructions:Bring a yoga mat and wear comfortable clothing suitable for movementRead More
Location (Bangalore only): Shoonya - Centre for Art and Somatic Practices

Name of the event: Melody in Motion - An Eve of Carnatic Violin Show
Event description: Melody in Motion is an evening dedicated to the art of Carnatic violin, presented by Soundstack in association with Artkhoj. At the heart of this concert is a rare and captivating violin duet by Vidwan G Ravi Kumar, a seasoned artist from the Mysore, known for his rich tonal depth, and Vidhushi Aishwarya Lakshman, a rising talent from Bengaluru celebrated for her perfectness.  Together, they bring a flowing conversation of Swara and Laya to the stage - an interplay that is both meditative and exhilarating. Accompanying them is Vidwan Balachandra Achar on the Mridangam, whose rhythmic precision and dynamic range add a powerful foundation to the concert, and Som Sankar Naskar on percussion, whose versatile accompaniment weaves seamlessly into the Carnatic idiom. Whether you are a seasoned Rasika or someone discovering Carnatic music for the first time, Melody in Motion promises an evening that stays with you long after the last note fades.Read More
Genre: carnatic, Indian Classical, Instrumental, Regional, Soul

Name of the event: Playful Connections - A two-day Clowning workshop
Event description: In a world that never stops trying to be perfect, what happens when you finally give yourself permission to be beautifully, brilliantly ridiculous?This April, we aren`t just celebrating the fool; we are decoding the quiet wisdom of the ridiculous. Playful Connections is a two-day introduction to the world of the red nose.This is for you if you`re a seeker:+ Performers, artists (of any kind) looking for a deeper creative process that helps make more courageous choices+ Teachers, activists, parents, students, corporate professionals who find themselves in stressful situations that feel heavy+ Therapists, doctors, healers who are keen about alternate ways of healing and reducing stress+ Anyone curious to find/refine their unique, funny self or looking for a laughter-filled weekendWORKSHOP DETAILS:Workshop Timing: 4 Apr and 5 Apr, Sat and Sun (10 a.m. - 5 p.m.) - 6 working hours per day + 1 hour for lunchLimited entries: 15 spots only.Age restriction: 16+ABOUT THE FACILITATORShivam is a Bangalore-based actor, facilitator, and clown whose artistic practice centers empathy, connection, and playfulness at the heart of the human experience.He has received formal training from Drama School Mumbai and has trained as a clown with international clown teachers practicing both healthcare as well as performative clowning.He has directed ‘Poor Vanya’ - a clown re-imagination of Anton Chekhov’s ‘Uncle Vanya’; and has also facilitated acting and clowning workshops at reputed institutions like the Drama School Mumbai and Ninasam.Shivam works as a healthcare clown with Dr. Parrots - a Bangalore-based organization that has touched over 5,000 lives through hospital visits, slum outreach, and NGO partnerships.FAQs1. Do I need to be ‘funny’ to attend?A resounding NO. Clowning is not just about being funny. It is about making a genuine, heartfelt connection with those around us. It is about finding your own unique humor through vulnerability and presence.2. I’m not really a performer. Is this for me?Absolutely. This is for everyone who is curious. Clowning is a realm of discovery, not a test of performing skill.3. What is the ‘Clown Spirit’?Briefly, it is the spirit of being present wholeheartedly and positively in the moment. The nose acts as a tool to help you stay open to everything there is to come."I remain just one thing, and one thing only, and that is a clown. It places me on a far higher plane than any politician." - Charlie ChaplinRead More

Can Indian CEOs be funny?Yes. Meet Atul Khatri, a middle-aged ex-CEO -tuned-comedian who quit his job in 2016 and now makes a living making people laugh harder than they ever have. Please watch his comedy special on Netflix as part of “Comedians of the World” which featured the globe’s top 47 comedians.Bringing his 55+-year-old perspective with every show, Atul starts this brand new show with how he is grateful to his audiences who buy tickets and come out to support live comedy. He then shares his experience about influencing and attending the “Wedding of the Century” alone without his wife. Atul then talks about how he lost 5 of his best friends who migrated to the USA after graduation and their stories whenever they visit India. He then talks about his experiences of how the USA has turned from the land of opportunities to the land of gratuities. His fun set includes his relationship with his 4-year-old grandson, his Indie dog Mr Butter Khatri who one day will have more Instagram followers than him. So, after 30+ years of marriage and surviving 2 children and 1 grand-child, Atul can proudly proclaim that he is “Well-Trained”CNN-IBN rated him as one of the Top Comedians of India. Join in as he makes fun of super-relatable observations and situations from all walks of life, truly making it a show for everyone and why he is known as the OG of the Indian Comedy Scene. Read More
